revision.comment: mentioning both the legacy EzPR and EzPR 2025.
revision.text:
{{xambox |image = [[File:Artículo bueno.svg|40px|link=]] |type = notice |id= review |text =
'''This article is waiting for review!'''
This article requires pre-publication review by an uninvolved reviewer (someone not substantially involved in writing the article). {{#ifexpr:{{PAGESINCATEGORY:Review}}>1|There are currently '''[[:Category:Review|{{PAGESINCATEGORY:Review}} articles]]''' in the review queue.|This is currently the only article in the review queue.}} If the article is accepted, it will be published; If declined, feedback will be posted on the [[{{TALKPAGENAME}}|talk page]]. You may continue improving the article while it awaits review.
'''While waiting, how you can help:'''
'''Self-check your article:''' * [[WN:Copyright|Copyright issues]] — No copyrighted text from sources * [[WN:Newsworthiness|Newsworthiness]] — Recent and relevant * [[WN:Source|Verifiability]] — Properly cited reliable sources * [[WN:NPOV|Neutrality]] — Balanced and unbiased * [[WN:Style guide|Style]] — Follows Wikinews conventions '''Help others get published:''' If your article is solid, consider giving another draft a [[Template:Pre-review|pre-review]]. Even small fixes help — proofreading, clarifying sources, or checking attribution. Collaborative review gets everyone's work published faster.
'''Instructions for Reviewers:'''
Only [[Wikinews:Reviewer|qualified reviewers]] may perform reviews and publish articles. To request reviewer rights, [[Wikinews:Flagged revisions/Requests for permissions|apply here]]. '''Review checklist:''' * Reviewers, please use the '''legacy Easy Peer Review''' ([[Template:Peer reviewed|documentation]]) or the '''Easy Peer Review 2025''' ([[WN:EzPR 2025|documentation]]) for reviews. * '''Last edited:''' {{#time:j M Y, G:i:s|{{REVISIONTIMESTAMP}}}} UTC ([{{fullurl:{{FULLPAGENAME}}|action=purge}} purge]) * Check [{{fullurl:{{TALKPAGENAME}}|action=history}} talk page history] for context * '''Copyright check:''' [https://copyvios.toolforge.org/?lang=en&project=wikinews&title={{FULLPAGENAMEE}}&oldid=&action=search&use_engine=1&use_links=1&turnitin=0 Earwig's copyvio tool]
{{Clickable button|Copyedit an Article|url=https://en.wikinews.org/wiki/{{FULLPAGENAMEE}}?withJS=MediaWiki:SubmitWizard.js&helpabandoned=1|class=mw-ui-progressive}}
{{Clickable button|Pre Review|url=https://en.wikinews.org/wiki/{{FULLPAGENAMEE}}?withJS=MediaWiki:SubmitWizard.js&pre-review=1|class=mw-ui-progressive}}
}} {{#if: {{{nocat|}}}||[[Category:Review]][[Category:Articles not disputed]]}}__NOINDEX__ {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Protected "[[Template:Published]]": I know this isn't the main template, but still, no one should ever move or touch this template. Ohh! shny new cascading protection button [edit=sysop:move=sysop]

revision.text:
See [[WN:REV]] for information on the relevant policy. {{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
---- This template sorts articles into [[:Category:Review]]. Some [[special:preferences|gadgets]] make special use of articles tagged with this template. If your article is time critical, consider adding {{tl|quick review}} or {{tl|breaking review}}.
It looks like this: {{review/live|nocat=demo}}
== Internals == The main template checks whether the request for review should be disabled (with an error message); if not disabled, it passes the request through to subtemplate {{tl|review/live}}.
<includeonly> <!-- CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS HERE, THANKS -->
[[Category:Article templates|Review]]
[[es:Plantilla:Revisar]] [[fa:الگو:مرور]] [[ja:Template:査読中]] [[tr:Şablon:Gözden geçir]] </includeonly>

revision.comment: suggest using (date of access) when no publication date is provided.
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{possible-risk}} ;See [[Wikinews:Style guide#Citing_your_references|Wikinews:Style guide]] for more information on citing your sources. === Usage === 

*{{source
 |url        = 
 |title      = 
 |author     = 
 |pub        = 
 |date       = 
 |archiveurl = 
}}
;Example 
*{{source
 |url        = https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-asia-58223231
 |title      = Afghanistan conflict: Taliban push into Kabul as Ghani flees
 |author     =
 |pub        = BBC News Online
 |date       = August 15, 2021
 |archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210815165730/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-58223231
}}
This will display as follows: *{{source |url = https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-asia-58223231 |title = Afghanistan conflict: Taliban push into Kabul as Ghani flees |author = |pub = BBC News Online |date = August 15, 2021 |archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20210815165730/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-58223231 }} *If you cannot find the author of a source, leave that field blank. Do not use 'staff reporter' or similar. List syndicate in the author field. Wikilink people and syndicates in this field when there's a target, local or non-local. *Put in a (second level) section labeled Sources (==Sources==). *Format date as shown above. If no publication date is provided, indicate the date the website was accessed, i.e., October 20, 2025 (date of access) *Always try to cite multiple independent sources. *Articles that don't provide a URL are listed at [[:Category:Articles with broken source templates]]. *[[:Category:Citation templates]] lists templates for offline news sources. *For unsupported URL schemas (like mms:) use ''brokenURL=true''. *For publications (the 'pub=' or 'publisher=' parameter) please specify the exact same name under which the publication appears on [[w:Main page|Wikipedia]]. *On those rare occasions when publication name should differ from publication link target, use additional parameter 'pubtarget='. You can also use this to specify a target project other than English Wikipedia. *For an unavailable source (''brokenURL=true'') of an archived article, you may provide information about archival copies of the source using 'archiveurl=' and 'archivedescription='. *Note: when ''brokenURL=true'', [archived version] appears, linking to archiveurl only ''after'' the article is protected. *For an author field in ALL CAPS, that cannot be remediated by means such as described [[Template:Source/ucauthor/doc#Avoiding this template|under subtemplate {{(*|2}}source/ucauthor{{*)|2}}]], and necessarily ''should'' be in ALL CAPS for reasons idiosyncratic to this particular citation rather than a general exception better addressed by {{tl|source/ucauthor}}, specify the precise acceptable value for the field as a 'cased author=' parameter. *For a non-English source, use the 'lang=' parameter, naming the language or its language code as supported by [[:Category:Language templates]] or corresponding language-code redirects. *For a video, use ''medium=video''. Also available, ''medium=audio'' (but as of this writing, not used anywhere), ''medium=pdf'', ''medium=word''. *For a source also available as a file on Wikimedia Commons, use the 'commons=' parameter, naming the file (without the File: prefix). ;Note: you should always cite more than one source. Do not list multiple publications with the same wire report; instead try to list the originating location. === See also === * {{tl|source/archived}} * {{tl|source/broken}} * {{tl|source/commons}} * {{tl|source/fixauthor}} * {{tl|source/lang}} * {{tl|source/medium}} * {{tl|source/nourl}} * {{tl|source/pub}} * {{tl|source/uc?}} * {{tl|source/ucauthor}} [[Category:Citation templates|Source]] [[ja:Template:Source-en]]
